examples.title: Pseudocode: Input Buffering
examples.description: Demonstrates the basic two-buffer scheme with sentinels
examples.code:
FUNCTION get_next_char():
 IF forward_ptr >= buffer_end:
 SWITCH TO OTHER BUFFER
 RELOAD EXHAUSTED BUFFER
 RESET forward_ptr TO START OF NEW BUFFER
 RETURN character AT forward_ptr++
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the basic logic for switching between two buffers and reloading the exhausted buffer when necessary.

examples.title: C: Input Buffering Implementation
examples.description: A simplified implementation of the two-buffer scheme with sentinels in C
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates the two-buffer scheme with sentinels. It uses two character arrays as buffers and switches between them when necessary.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for get_next_char(), O(n) for load_buffer() where n is the buffer size
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is the buffer size

examples.title: Python: Input Buffering Implementation
examples.description: A simplified implementation of the two-buffer scheme with sentinels in Python
examples.code:
BUFFER_SIZE = 4096
SENTINEL = '\0'

buffer1 = bytearray(BUFFER_SIZE)
buffer2 = bytearray(BUFFER_SIZE)
current_buffer = buffer1
forward_ptr = 0
current_buffer_num = 1


def get_next_char():
 global forward_ptr, current_buffer, current_buffer_num
 if current_buffer[forward_ptr] == ord(SENTINEL):
 # Switch buffers
 if current_buffer_num == 1:
 load_buffer(buffer2)
 current_buffer = buffer2
 current_buffer_num = 2
 forward_ptr = 0
 else:
 load_buffer(buffer1)
 current_buffer = buffer1
 current_buffer_num = 1
 forward_ptr = 0
 char = chr(current_buffer[forward_ptr])
 forward_ptr += 1
 return char


def load_buffer(buffer):
 # Read from input file into buffer
 # Add sentinel at the end
 with open('input_file', 'rb') as f:
 bytes_read = f.readinto(buffer)
 buffer[bytes_read] = ord(SENTINEL)
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the two-buffer scheme with sentinels. It uses two bytearray objects as buffers and switches between them when necessary.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for get_next_char(), O(n) for load_buffer() where n is the buffer size
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is the buffer size
